text: When you are writing code, finding and fixing errors, or "bugs," is part of the process. The first step in debugging is identifying the bug. This means understanding what the program should do versus what it actually does. A systematic approach helps here: instead of guessing, test one small part of your code at a time. Use debugging tools, like a debugger in your code editor, to pause the program and inspect variables. Another useful tool is logging. By adding log messages to different parts of your code, you can see the flow of data and spot where things go wrong. With practice, debugging becomes easier and faster.
